name: Sazerac Rouge, Blanc, Bleu Sorbets in Ice Swa
recipeIngredient: 1 large egg white, beaten until stiff peaks form
2 pints fresh or 4 cups frozen blackberries
2 large egg whites, beaten until stiff peaks form
recipeInstructions:
Servings: 12
For elegance and drama, borrow the Sazerac tradition of serving colorful sorbets in a glistening swan ice sculpture. The ice mold, which is truly impressive, is easy to create with a soft-plastic mold filled with distilled water and frozen overnight. The next day, simply pull off the plastic.
Info on ordering the mold is included at the end of the recipe.
If using an ice swan, prepare several days in advance. Fill the mold with distilled water. Freeze until firm throughout. Remove from the mold and return to freezer until ready to use.
To make Lemon Sorbet:
1. In a 2-quart saucepan, heat water and sugar to boiling; boil mixture for 5 minutes.
2. Cool sugar mixture to room temperature.
3. Stir in mineral water, lemon juice, and rind.
4. Gently fold in egg white.
5. Freeze according to Freezing Directions (below).
To make Strawberry Sorbet:
1. In a 2-quart saucepan, heat sugar and water to boiling; boil mixture for 5 minutes.
2. Cool sugar mixture to room temperature.
3. In a food processor with chopping blade, puree strawberries, one cup at a time.
4. Strain juice and pulp into a bowl; discard seeds.
5. Stir strawberry puree and orange and lemon juices into sugar mixture.
6. Freeze according to Freezing Directions (below).
To make Blackberry Sorbet:
1. In a 2-quart saucepan, heat sugar and water to boiling; boil mixture for 5 minutes.
2. Cool sugar mixture to room temperature.
3. In a food processor with chopping blade, puree blackberries, one cup at a time.
4. Strain juice and pulp into a bowl; discard seeds.
5. Gently fold blackberry puree and egg whites into sugar mixture.
6. Freeze according to Freezing Directions (below).
Just before serving, place the swan on a cloth napkin-lined serving platter and garnish with mint and flowers, if desired. Scoop sorbets into the swan or a glass bowl and serve immediately.
Freezing Directions:
If using an ice cream maker, freeze the sorbet mixture following manufacturer's directions. If not using an ice cream maker, pour the sorbet mixture into a 13 by 9 inch baking pan; cover and freeze 6 to 8 hours or until frozen. Remove sorbet from freezer; let stand at room temperature for 20 minutes or until slightly softened. Meanwhile, chill a large bowl and beaters of an electric mixer. Place softened sorbet squares in the chilled bowl. Beat on low speed until squares are broken up, then beat on high speed until smooth and fluffy (about 1 minute). Pack sorbet into a freezer container and freeze until ready to serve.
Note: To obtain Swan Mold, call RMI-D, Ice Creations Unlimited at: (303) 373-5001. Ice molds are $37.00 plus shipping and handling (as of July, 1990).
Source: Country Living magazine, 07/90
From: Sallie Austin
